description: Legal query. Dating: Early 13th century (per Goitein). Concerning the wife of a Kohen who traveled two or three times without letting her know how long he would remain absent or leaving her sufficient food. She swore "oaths frightening the mountains" (אימאן תרעד אלגבאל) that after his return she would not remain with him. The fact of his being a kohen is mentioned because a kohen is forbidden to marry a divorcée. It was common for couples to divorce and then remarry, so (as Friedman points out) "the sages were reluctant to sanction a Kohen's divorce and attempted to delay such actions (see, e.g., M. Ket. 7:1)." The empty space at the bottom of the page was probably left to write the responsum. (Information from Goitein's note card and from Friedman, "Divorce upon the Wife's Demand.")
description_he: שאלה הלכתית. תחילת המאה ה-13. עוסקת באשת כהן שנסע מספר פעמים מבלי להודיעה כמה זמן יהיה נעדר. היא נשבעה שאחרי שובו לא תישאר עמו. הכהן אסור לשאת גרושה.
